Author: SnowRadiance

Name: Refill Jetpack with Oil
Description:
[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/color]
Jetpack Refuel adds a new way to maintain your Jetpack.
Hold CTRL and drag an oil-filled liquid container onto a Jetpack to open a fuel transfer window. Transfer oil into the Jetpack to restore its condition.
The amount of durability restored depends on how much oil is transferred.

Current balance:
100 mL of oil = 10% Jetpack condition restored


[b][color=#ff7700][size=4]Installation instructions[/size][/color][/b]
1. Install BepInEx. and Mod
2. Extract "mod.dll" into:
Casualties Unknown Demo\BepInEx\plugins

3. Launch the game.

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Refuel Jetpacks using Oil. [/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]To refuel a Jetpack:
• Fill a liquid container with oil.
• Hold CTRL.
• Drag the oil container onto the Jetpack. [/color]

[b][color=#ff7700][size=4][Multiplay][/size][/color]
if you want to use this mod on the client side in multiplayer, the host must have the mod installed.
but if only the host installs the mod, clients can still play without any issues, but the mod features won't function for them.[/b]

If you run into any issues with larger groups or find any bugs, please leave a post and let me know! xd

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size][/color]
[size=4][b][color=#ffffff]• [/color][/b][/size][b][color=#d4d4d8][url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/]BepInEx[/url][/color][/b][color=#d4d4d8] For mods![/color]



[color=#d4d4d8]Mod idea & Request by.Ohyaji
[/color]
Author: Elysian0166

Name: Sanguinite Weaponry
Description:
[b][size=6]Overview:[/size]
[/b]
[size=4]Sanguinite Weaponry aims to add balanced blood-themed weapons/tools that are higher in tier above titanium. they are more durable, more specialized and very slowly restore their durability over time.[/size]
[size=4]The main component of sanguinite is red blood and titanium.[/size][b][size=4] [/size][/b][size=4]Due to the natural scarcity of red blood a custom crafting component, the "blood precursor device" was added to aid in its obtainment, allowing you to craft red blood through a balanced crafting recipie.
+ different random weapons/tools and items I felt like making and adding :3[/size]

[size=5][b]Important![/b][/size]
[size=4]Due to the fact that titanium only spawns in layer 5 as of 7.0.0 a mod called "[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/214][color=#3c78d8]Titanium Restored[/color][/url]" is recommended to use alongside this mod.[/size]

[size=6][b]Mod Contents:[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]New weapons / tools:[/b][/size]
[color=#ffffff][size=4]-[/size][/color][color=#ff0000][size=4] sanguinite [/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=4]saber[/size][/color]
[color=#ffffff][size=4]-[/size][/color][color=#cc0000][size=4] sanguinite [/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=4]claws[/size][/color]
[color=#ffffff][size=4]-[/size][/color][color=#ff0000][size=4] sanguinite [/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=4]scythe[/size][/color]
[size=4][color=#ffffff]-[/color][color=#cc0000] sanguinite [/color][color=#ffffff]warhammer[/color]
[color=#ffffff]- the[/color][color=#ff0000] artery[/color]
[color=#ffffff]-[/color][color=#a4c2f4] titanium [/color][color=#ffffff]pocket knife[/color][/size]
[color=#ffffff][size=4]-[/size][/color][color=#6aa84f][size=4] cutting-edge technology[/size][/color]
[b][size=4]New utilities:
[/size][/b][color=#ffffff][size=4]-[/size][/color][size=4][color=#ff0000] brimflame [/color][/size][color=#ffffff][size=4]lighter[/size][/color] [size=1][color=#ffffff](shoutout to[/color][color=#ff0000] [/color][url=https://forums.nexusmods.com/profile/194640039-comradefoxx/][color=#ff7700]comradefoxx[/color][/url] for helping with coding this one <3 )[/size]
[b][size=4]Misc items:[/size][/b]
[color=#ffffff][size=4]-[/size][/color][color=#ff0000][size=4] sanguinite [/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=4]blob[/size][/color]
[size=4][color=#ffffff]-[/color][color=#ff0000] blood[/color] [color=#dd7e6b]precursor[/color] [color=#ffd966]device
[/color][/size]
[size=6][b]Weapon attributes list:[/b][/size]

[size=4]- [color=#ff00ff]short[/color]: the weapon has less range than usual.[/size]
[size=4]- [color=#00ff00]long[/color]: the weapon has more range than usual.
- [color=#00ffff]nimble[/color]: the weapon is faster than usual.
- [color=#ff7700]heavy[/color]: the weapon is slower than usual. [/size]
[size=4]- [color=#9900ff]piercing[/color]: the weapon can hit multiple targets at once.
- [color=#999999]metal crusher[/color]: the weapon deals x10 damage to metal tiles.[/size]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
If you mod your game for first time (dont have BepInEx):
[spoiler]Show
Go to BepInEx download page
https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/tag/v5.4.23.5
And download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5
Open steam, choose browse local file
Extract BepInEx contents into game folder's root (winhttp.dll should be in same folder as executable)
Open game once and close
Now folder BepInEx/plugins should be created[/spoiler]
If you already have BepInEx isnstalled then extract the contents of the zip. file into these specific folders:
extract "SanguiniteWeaponry" into the BepInEx/plugins folder
extract "CraftingFramework" into the BepInEx/plugins folder
(if you already have CraftingFramework just move the recipe file into the already existing folder)
Author: wictorias

Name: Better Crafting Logic
Description:
[img]https://github.com/BalaurBohemianBroken/cu-better-inventory-logic/blob/master/PageImages/cu-summary.png?raw=true[/img]
[color=#ff0000][size=2][u]Why[/u][/size][/color][size=2] are you ripping up your shirt when you have a canvas??[/size]
[size=2]Provides a more intelligent system for choosing crafting materials, and for storing crafted items.[/size]


[img]https://github.com/BalaurBohemianBroken/cu-better-inventory-logic/blob/master/PageImages/cu-features.png?raw=true[/img]
[size=2]• Choose suitable crafting materials based on quality, condition, value.[/size]
[size=2]• Use clothing and containers as materials only as a last resort.[/size]
[size=2]• Store crafted materials more intelligently! Put materials in the [color=#ff0000][u]hecking[/u][/color] materials pouch![/size]
[size=2]• A helpful pop up that informs you of where an item was stored.[/size]
[size=2]• Settings, under the [b]Gameplay[/b] tab. Every feature and filter can be individually disabled![/size]


[img]https://github.com/BalaurBohemianBroken/cu-better-inventory-logic/blob/master/PageImages/cu-installation.png?raw=true[/img]
[size=2]Same dealio as most mods. BepInEx, place .dll in plugins.[/size]
[size=2]BepInEx install steps:[/size]
[spoiler][color=#D4D4D8][size=2]1. Download [/size][/color][color=#8e7cc3][size=2][font=Courier New][b]BepInEx_win_x64[/b][/font][/size][/color][color=#D4D4D8][size=2] (even if you're on [/size][/color][color=#ff00ff][size=2]Linux[/size][/color][color=#D4D4D8][size=2]) from their [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases]releases[/url]. I used version [/size][/color][color=#8e7cc3][size=2][b]5.4.23.4[/b][/size][/color][color=#D4D4D8][size=2].

2. Unzip the file contents into the root folder of the game. In the same directory as the [/size][/color][color=#8e7cc3][size=2][b].exe[/b][/size][/color][color=#D4D4D8][size=2] file, there should be a [/size][/color][color=#8e7cc3][size=2][b]winhttp.dll[/b][/size][/color][color=#D4D4D8][size=2] file if you've done it right.

2.5. [/size][/color][size=2][b][color=#ff00ff]Linux users[/color][/b][color=#D4D4D8][b]:[/b] Add the following command to your launch options, on steam found in Properties > General > Launch Options: [/color][b][color=#6d9eeb][font=Courier New]WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%[/font][/color][/b][color=#D4D4D8]

3. Launch the game. This will initialize BepInEx. If it worked, in Casualties Unknown Demo > BepInEx, there should exist a [/color][color=#8e7cc3][b]plugins[/b][/color][color=#D4D4D8] folder.[/color][/size][/spoiler]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=2]From there, place the provided [/size][/color][color=#8e7cc3][size=2][b].dll[/b][/size][/color][color=#D4D4D8][size=2] file into [b]BepInEx/plugins[/b][/size][/color]


[img]https://github.com/BalaurBohemianBroken/cu-better-inventory-logic/blob/master/PageImages/cu-feature-ideas.png?raw=true[/img]
[size=2]• Automatically craft prerequisites for recipes.[/size]
[size=2]• Multiplayer support......[/size]
[size=2]• Click on chosen material in crafting menu to exclude that material, and find a new one.[/size]
[size=2]• Craft using mixed liquids first.[/size]
[size=2]• Top off bottles that are partially filled, and move on to filling a new bottle with the remainder of a recipe.[/size]
[size=2]• Allow recipes to use containers (like bags) which have items in them, with a warning.[/size]
[size=2]• Show more information about input and output items in crafting menu[/size]
[size=2]• Rearranging the sorting stack, to choose what is the most important factor to you.[/size]


[img]https://github.com/BalaurBohemianBroken/cu-better-inventory-logic/blob/master/PageImages/cu-compatibility.png?raw=true[/img]
[size=2]Works with existing saves.[/size]

[size=2]Not currently compatible with multiplayer mods. I've not grabbed someone into playing it with me, so I haven't had the motivation yet![/size]

[size=2]Compatible with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/7]QoL Unknown[/url] as of now. I will try to maintain compatibility if more features are added.[/size]

[b][color=#8e7cc3][size=4]Bug reporting[/size][/color][/b]
[size=2]There may be bugs I haven't noticed! I've hit a rogue wave of depression and haven't been able to do thorough testing. If you find a bug, let me know and I'll try to fix it quickly. Guide for that:[/size]
[spoiler][size=2]The more information is provided, the quicker developers are able to address the issue!
Any information is always better than no information. Even if you can't get much, making any post may help.[/size]
[size=2]Bug reports should go into the [b]Bugs[/b] section of the mod page.

[b]Description[/b] - Details of what was expected to happen, and what happened instead. Any information that might suggest what the cause is. It can't be fixed if we don't know what the issue is!

[b]Replicability[/b] - If you can give steps to reliably reproduce the issue, that's the most helpful thing! It makes it quick to verify the issue and test fixes.

[b]Existing bug reports[/b] - Try to check if there's already been a report of this bug. You could add more information to that report, or find an explanation of what's going on.

[b]Game version[/b] - This can be seen on the main menu in the bottom right. Code can change a lot between versions!

[b]Log file[/b] - This is located in {game_folder}/BepInEx/LogOutput.log. It provides a list of your mods and their versions, which are very helpful for tracking down the issue!

[b]Updating[/b] - Try to make sure your mods and game are up to date, especially the mod that has the bug. It might have been changed, or fixed.

Modding is not simple work, and most of us do this for free out of passion in our spare time. Be considerate. It might take time to fix things.[/size][/spoiler]

Name: Performance Unknown
Description:
[size=6][b]Performance Unknown[/b][/size]

An advanced, hardware-aligned performance and memory allocation optimization BepInEx mod for [b]Casualties Unknown[/b]. It tackles major Unity-specific bottlenecks (heavy line-of-sight raycasting, dynamic 2D physics solvers, memory allocation stutters, and splatter particle spam) and exposes high-level, human-readable quality adjustments directly inside the official native Video Settings menu using the [font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font] API.

[line]

[size=5][b]🚀 Key Features & Optimizations[/b][/size]

[list]
[*] [b]📷 LOS Refresh Amortization:[/b] Skips Line-of-Sight raycast loops in [font=Courier New]LateUpdate[/font] across multiple frames based on dynamic settings, yielding a [b]50% - 75% reduction in CPU overhead[/b] with zero visual compromise.[/*]
[*][b]🩸 Splatter Particle Ceiling:[/b] Implements an active FIFO-pruning system on visual blood splatters, dust, debris, and explosions to prevent combat framerate drops and massive rendering bottlenecks.[/*]
[*][b]⚙️ Physics 2D Solver Tuning:[/b] Dynamically adjusts Box2D position and velocity solver iterations, [b]drastically speeding up crowded scenes[/b] with multiple active NPCs, ragdolls, or items.[/*]
[*][b]🧹 Stutter-Free Garbage Collection (GC):[/b] Allocates incremental GC timeslices to split heap sweeps across frames, and executes preemptive safe garbage sweeping when entering paused states or loading scenes to eliminate micro-stutters.[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[size=5][b]📊 Native Video Settings Integration[/b][/size]

Through the power of [font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font], all configurations are integrated directly into the game's official native Video Settings menu, enabling fluid in-game changes without restart:







[table][tr][td][b]Setting Name[/b][/td]
[td][b]Category[/b][/td]
[td][b]Choices / Defaults[/b][/td]
[td][b]Optimization Vector[/b][/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]LOS Refresh Interval[/b][/td]
[td]Video[/td]
[td][font=Courier New]Every Frame[/font] / [font=Courier New]Every 2 Frames[/font] (Fast) / [font=Courier New]Every 3 Frames[/font] (Ultra) / [font=Courier New]Every 4 Frames[/font][/td]
[td]Amortizes line-of-sight raycasts to skip intensive trigonometry calculations.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Splatter Particle Cap[/b][/td]
[td]Video[/td]
[td][font=Courier New]25[/font] / [font=Courier New]50[/font] / [font=Courier New]100[/font] (Balanced) / [font=Courier New]200[/font] / [font=Courier New]Unlimited[/font][/td]
[td]Active FIFO-limiting of splatters and visual particles.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Physics 2D Solver Quality[/b][/td]
[td]Video[/td]
[td][font=Courier New]Low[/font] / [font=Courier New]Medium[/font] (Optimized) / [font=Courier New]High[/font] (Default)[/td]
[td]Tunes velocity and position solver iterations.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]GC Memory Sweeper[/b][/td]
[td]Video[/td]
[td][font=Courier New]Enabled[/font] (Default) / [font=Courier New]Disabled[/font][/td]
[td]Tunes incremental GC timeslices and sweeps on safe events.[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[line]

[size=5][b]🛠️ Requirements & Installation[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*] Ensure you have [b][url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx]BepInEx[/url][/b] (5.x or later) installed for the game.[/*]
[*]Ensure you have [b][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/b] (1.0.0 or later) installed in your [font=Courier New]BepInEx/plugins[/font] folder.[/*]
[*]Download the latest [font=Courier New]UnknownPerformance.dll[/font] from the releases.[/*]
[*]Place [font=Courier New]UnknownPerformance.dll[/font] into your [font=Courier New]BepInEx/plugins/[/font] folder.[/*]
[/list]
Author: NaeNaeTart

Name: CUCoreLib
Description:
[center][size=6][color=#f39c12][b]CUCoreLib[/b][/color][/size]
[size=4][color=#dfe6e9]A definitive open-source modding library for Casualties: Unknown.[/color][/size]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24e022ba-0af1-4ce9-a66c-6b28b5e941f1[/img]

[url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/CUCoreLib/releases][img]https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/jimmyking9999999/CUCoreLib?color=orange&logo=github[/img][/url] [url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/CUCoreLib/releases][img]https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/jimmyking9999999/CUCoreLib/total?color=blue&logo=github[/img][/url] [img]https://img.shields.io/badge/Game_version-v7.1.0-green[/img] [url=https://cucorelib.web.app/#welcome][img]https://img.shields.io/badge/Documentation-blue[/img][/url][/center]
[line]
[color=#f5cba7][i]"awwww dang it @jimmy_king im not trusting you with my code again ;;!!!"[/i][/color]
[color=#b2bec3]- Moffee, game creator[/color]

[left][color=#55efc4][b]This clearly means you should trust my library with your mod :)[/b][/color][/left]
[line]
[b][color=#f39c12][size=5]What Is CUCoreLib?[/size][/color][/b]

CUCoreLib is an [b]exhaustive shared modding library and documentation project[/b] for Casualties: Unknown, built for both [b]beginners[/b] and [b]veteran mod authors[/b].

Instead of every mod reinventing the same systems over and over, CUCoreLib provides a common foundation, with documentation.

[color=#74b9ff][b]We have:[/b][/color]
[list]
[*]A human-written shared framework for building mods faster[/*]
[*]A safer compatibility layer for future modding work and multi-mod compatibility (it works with QoL and Multiplayer off the bat!)[/*]
[*]A very well documented API surface with examples and videos ([url=https://cucorelib.jimmyking.dev]cucorelib.jimmyking.dev[/url])[/*]
[*]A open-source project intended to grow with community feedback and contributions[/*]
[*]Hot loading, so you don't have to close your game every time you want to update your mod (!!)[/*]
[/list]
[color=#f39c12][size=5][b]What Does It Provide?[/b][/size][/color]

[color=#ffeaa7][b]Core library features[/b][/color]
[list]
[*]Helper functions and shared utility systems[/*]
[*]Publicized access to useful private game data[/*]
[*]Mod-compatible patch hooks[/*]
[*]Documentation through an external webapp with examples, references, and videos[/*]
[*]Makes modding much faster and streamlined[/*]
[/list]
[color=#ffeaa7][b]In-game modular API support[/b][/color]
[list]
[*]Custom items, liquids, containers, weapons, crafting materials, drops, and more[/*]
[*]Equipment[/*]
[*]Moodles and custom status effects[/*]
[*]Crafting and recipes[/*]
[*]Console commands[/*]
[*]Automatic Locale and translation support[/*]
[*]Settings menu support[/*]
[*]Saving and persistent mod data[/*]
[*]Custom world entities, tiles, traps, and interactables[/*]
[*]Multiplayer API and helpers[/*]
[*]General utility systems for mod authors[/*]
[/list]
[color=#f39c12][size=5][b]Why Use It?[/b][/size][/color]

A lot of mods need the same basic foundation:
[list]
[*]Loading custom assets[/*]
[*]Registering items, liquids, recipes, and world content[/*]
[*]Saving mod data safely[/*]
[*]Patching game systems without exploding on updates[/*]
[*]Staying compatible with other mods[/*]
[*]Handling multiplayer-related integration[/*]
[/list]
Without a shared library, every mod author ends up building their own version of the same systems. That makes mods harder to maintain, harder to update, more likely to conflict, and more annoying to extend later.

[center][color=#55efc4]CUCoreLib aims to become a common framework the modding scene can build on together.[/color][/center]

[center][color=#ff7675][b]! This library is fully open source and open to pull requests! ![/b][/color][/center]

[line]

Name: ScavSetLib
Description:
[size=6][b][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url] ([url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSettingsLib[/url]) — Official API Reference[/b][/size]

[font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font] is an independent, highly optimized class library/API designed for BepInEx mod developers of [b]Casualties Unknown[/b]. It allows mods to register custom settings (Dropdowns, Floats, Integers, or Booleans) directly inside the game's official menus (Video, Audio, Game, etc.) with native styling, native sliders, native toggle switches, and fully automatic loading, saving, and localization lookup overrides.

[line]

[size=5][b]1. Core Technical Architecture[/b][/size]

The library bypasses the need for every developer to write redundant Harmony patches on the game's settings template lists and [font=Courier New]Locale[/font] translations. Instead, it runs a single centralized patch system that dynamically polls custom setting definitions and resolves their visual naming at runtime.

[b]System Workflow:[/b]
[code]
Mod Developers
  │
  └── (Call Register API) ──> SettingsManager
                                │
                                └──> Populates Definitions & Localizations
                                      │
                                      └──> Registry Maps
                                              │
      ┌──────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┐
      ▼                                      ▼                                      ▼
Settings.DefaultSettings Postfix      Settings.EnsureLoaded Postfix          Locale.GetString Prefix
(Hook Default Templates)              (Hook Live Settings)                  (Hook Localization)
      │                                      │                                      │
      ▼                                      ▼                                      ▼
Instantiates Custom Settings          Instantiates / Syncs Settings          Overrides 'gameset' strings
      │                                      │                                      │
      └──────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┘
                                              ▼
                                    Game's Native UI Renderer[/code]
[line]

[size=5][b]2. API Structuring[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]2.1 Supported Setting Types[/b][/size]
[list]
[*] [b]Dropdowns ([font=Courier New]SettingDropdown[/font]):[/b] Selection menus with clean option labels.[/*]
[*][b]Float Sliders ([font=Courier New]SettingFloat[/font]):[/b] Float values with customizable range (min/max) and string formatting (e.g. percentages, decimals).[/*]
[*][b]Integer Sliders ([font=Courier New]SettingInt[/font]):[/b] Integer step-based ranges (min/max).[/*]
[*][b]Toggle Switches ([font=Courier New]SettingBool[/font]):[/b] Standard true/false checkboxes.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]2.2 Base Setting Definition[/b][/size]
All setting types inherit from a base definition class that populates core fields such as internal name, category (Video, Audio, Game, Input, Language), and synchronizes values when modified elsewhere.

[code]
public abstract class SettingDefinition
{
    public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    public Setting.SettingCategory Category { get; set; }
    public string CleanName { get; set; } = string.Empty;

    public abstract Setting CreateSettingInstance();
    public abstract void SyncValueFromSource(Setting setting);
}[/code]
[line]

[size=5][b]3. How to Use [url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url] in Your Mod[/b][/size]

[b]Step 1: Declare the BepInEx Dependency[/b]
Ensure BepInEx is aware that your mod depends on [font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font] so it loads in the correct order:
[code]
[BepInPlugin("com.yourname.yourmod", "YourMod", "1.0.0")]
[BepInDependency("com.kanisuko.scavsetlib")]
public class YourPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin
{
    // ...
}[/code]
[b]Step 2: Register Settings inside [font=Courier New]Awake()[/font][/b]
To inject custom settings, call the static [font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url].SettingsManager[/font] registration methods during initialization.

[code]
using ScavSetLib;

private void Awake()
{
    // 1. Register a Dropdown setting inside the native VIDEO category
    SettingsManager.RegisterDropdown(
        name: "MyCustomMode",
        category: Setting.SettingCategory.Video,
        choices: new string[] { "Off", "Low", "Ultra" },
        defaultValue: 0,
        onApply: (selectedVal) => {
            // Callback when modified in-game
            MyConfig.CustomMode.Value = selectedVal;
        },
        valueGetter: () => MyConfig.CustomMode.Value, // For active synchronization
        cleanName: "Ultra Visual Mode",              // Clean display label
        cleanChoiceNames: new string[] { "Disabled", "Standard Quality", "Extreme Ultra" } // Clean options
    );

    // 2. Register a Slider setting inside the native GAME category
    SettingsManager.RegisterFloat(
        name: "MyCustomSpeed",
        category: Setting.SettingCategory.Game,
        min: 0.5f,
        max: 2.0f,
        defaultValue: 1.0f,
        onApply: (speedVal) => {
            MyConfig.GameSpeed.Value = speedVal;
        },
        valueGetter: () => MyConfig.GameSpeed.Value,
        cleanName: "Simulation Velocity",
        formatValue: (val) => $"{val:F1}x" // Formats float slider label natively
    );
}[/code]
[line]

[size=5][b]4. Under the Hood Mechanics[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]4.1 Native UI Rendering Hooking[/b][/size]
When [font=Courier New]SettingsMenu.SelectTab(SettingCategory)[/font] is invoked, it instantiates option prefabs by looping through [font=Courier New]Settings.settings[/font]. Because [font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font] injects your settings directly into [font=Courier New]Settings.settings[/font] (and matches values loaded from save files), the game [b]automatically creates and renders native GameObjects[/b] (sliders, toggles, dropdown boxes) using its built-in styling, saving you hours of manual UI development!

[size=4][b]4.2 Bulletproof Localization Interception[/b][/size]
The game automatically prepends [font=Courier New]"gameset"[/font] to unregistered options when fetching localizations, which would normally result in broken text (e.g., [font=Courier New]"gamesetUltra Visual Mode"[/font]).

To prevent this, [font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font] intercepts [font=Courier New]Locale.GetString(string str, int type)[/font] with a [font=Courier New]HarmonyPrefix[/font] and does a high-performance O(1) dictionary lookup to immediately swap prefix strings:

[code]
[HarmonyPatch(typeof(Locale), "GetString")]
[HarmonyPrefix]
public static bool GetString_Prefix(string str, int type, ref string __result)
{
    if (str != null && SettingsManager.TryGetLocalization(str, out string localized))
    {
        __result = localized;
        return false; // Skip original game dictionary lookup
    }
    return true; // Default fallback for native game strings
}[/code]
This guarantees:
[list=1]
[*] [b]Dynamic Custom Labeling:[/b] Perfectly matches clean strings provided by developers.[/*]
[*][b]No Wrapping Anomalies:[/b] Choice items in dropdown lists display properly formatted text, eliminating ugly text wrapping.[/*]
[*][b]Zero Overhead:[/b] The dictionary check runs instantly and has negligible performance impact on original game translation files.[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[quote][b]NOTE[/b]
[font=Courier New][url=https://github.com/NaeNaeTart/ScavSetLib]ScavSetLib[/url][/font] operates as a pure, standalone, dependency-free API, requiring only basic Unity/BepInEx libraries.[/quote]
Author: NaeNaeTart

Name: More Trader Types
Description:
[size=6][b]More Traders Mod[/b][/size]

A BepInEx mod for [b]Casualties Unknown[/b] that adds custom, fully-featured trading NPCs to the game. Complete with in-game developer console integration, autocompletion support, and built-in anti-exploit protection.

[line]

[size=5][b]👥 Meet the Traders[/b][/size]

This mod introduces three unique non-playable characters (NPCs) with custom trading inventories, names, and interaction behaviors:

[size=4][b]🩺 Dr. Hernandez (Medic)[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Role:[/b] Specialized medical supplier and practitioner.[/*]
[*][b]Service:[/b] Automatically restores your health to full when you initiate a trade with him.[/*]
[*][b]Inventory:[/b] Sells vital medical supplies, first-aid kits, and medicine to help you survive.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🍳 Mama Cecile (Chef)[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Role:[/b] Culinary expert and restaurant owner.[/*]
[*][b]Service:[/b] Automatically satisfies your hunger and increases player satisfaction upon opening her trade menu.[/*]
[*][b]Inventory:[/b] Sells ingredients, canned goods, and home-cooked meals.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🎲 Lucky (Gambler)[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Role:[/b] High-roller dealer.[/*]
[*][b]Service:[/b] Tests your luck with mystery boxes and lottery-style items.[/*]
[*][b]Inventory:[/b] Sells custom mystery boxes, game tickets, and unique high-risk high-reward items.[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[size=5][b]💻 Console Commands & Autocomplete[/b][/size]

Spawn these traders instantly anywhere using the in-game developer console.






[table][tr][td][b]Command[/b][/td]
[td][b]Action[/b][/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][i]spawnmedic[/i][/td]
[td]Spawns [b]Dr. Hernandez[/b] (Medic) at your current crosshair position.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][i]spawnchef[/i][/td]
[td]Spawns [b]Mama Cecile[/b] (Chef) at your current crosshair position.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][i]spawngambler[/i][/td]
[td]Spawns [b]Lucky[/b] (Gambler) at your current crosshair position.[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[size=4][b]⚡ Autocomplete Support[/b][/size]
Type [i]spawn[/i] in the developer console and press [i]Tab[/i] to quickly browse and auto-complete these commands!

[line]

[size=5][b]🛠️ Advanced Features[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]🛡️ Anti-Exploit State Tracker[/b][/size]
To prevent players from repeatedly opening and closing trading menus to infinitely heal or feed themselves, the mod includes a robust state tracking system:
[list]
[*][b]Interaction Cooldown:[/b] A trader's special status action (healing for Dr. Hernandez, feeding for Mama Cecile) will only trigger [b]once[/b] per spawn or per long interaction cycle.[/*]
[*][b]Fair Play:[/b] Closing and reopening the menu immediately on the same NPC will not re-trigger the benefit, keeping the game balanced.[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[size=5][b]📥 Installation[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*][b]Prerequisite:[/b] Ensure you have [b]BepInEx[/b] installed for [i]Casualties Unknown[/i].[/*]
[*][b]Download:[/b] Grab the latest [i]MoreTraders.dll[/i] from the Releases tab.[/*]
[*][b]Deploy:[/b] Place the downloaded [i]MoreTraders.dll[/i] file into your game's plugins directory:
[i][Casualties Unknown Game Directory]/BepInEx/plugins/[/i][/*]
[*][b]Play:[/b] Start the game and enjoy![/*]
[/list]
Author: NaeNaeTart

Name: LateJoinFix
Description:
### Late Join Fix

A small **optional** BepInEx plugin for **Casualties Unknown** multiplayer.

It is **not** a replacement for [Casualties Together](https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67) — you must install Krokosha’s MP mod first. Late Join Fix only addresses **late-join session inventory**: players who join mid-run can receive the host’s session save **once**, without duplicate grants after disconnect/reconnect.

---

### Requirements

| Required | Notes |
|----------|--------|
| **Casualties Unknown** | Current Steam demo / game build |
| **BepInEx 5.x** | Included with Casualties Together setup |
| **[Casualties Together](https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67)** | **v3.1.2** — by **Krokosha** |
| **All players** | Host and every client need this file |

**Hard dependency:** `KrokoshaCasualtiesMP` (Casualties Together).

---

### What it does

- Host archives per-player saves during the session (on save/load).
- **First-time** late joiners receive their session inventory snapshot when they spawn.
- Players who already received a grant, or left and rejoined the same session, do **not** get a second grant.
- Clears disconnect reconnect saves for players who already consumed their grant.

This is a **standalone, focused** release of the original community late-join save fix. It does **not** include layer unlocks, terrain changes, connection/Steam relay patches, or other gameplay overhauls.

---

### Installation

1. Install **[Casualties Together](https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67)** (3.1.2) and confirm multiplayer works.
2. Extract so you have:
BepInEx/plugins/LateJoinFix/LateJoinFix.dll
3. Install on **host and all clients**.
4. Do **not** use alongside other late-join / session-save patches (only one such mod at a time).

---

### Compatibility

- Built for **Casualties Together 3.1.2** only.
- **Not** affiliated with, endorsed by, or maintained by **Krokosha** or the Casualties Unknown developers.
- Not a fork of Casualties Together — install it **in addition to** the MP mod.

---

### Credits

- **[Casualties Together](https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67)** — **Krokosha** (required multiplayer framework)
- Late-join session save logic — community fix for Casualties Together 3.1.2; this release is a standalone rebuild as **Late Join Fix**
- Nexus upload / maintenance — LYSJca

---

### 中文说明

本模组是 **Casualties Unknown** 联机的**可选补丁**，依赖 Krokosha 的 **[Casualties Together](https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67)**（**3.1.2**），不能替代联机本体。

**作用：** 修复中途加入时会话背包发放——首次加入可领取主机会话存档，已领取或中途离开再回来的玩家不会重复发放。

**安装：** `BepInEx/plugins/LateJoinFix/LateJoinFix.dll`，主客机均需安装。

**注意：** 与 Casualties Together 无隶属关系；勿与其他中途加入存档类模组叠用。
Author: LYSJca

Name: Interactive Expie
Description:
[size=6][b]🐾 Expie Petting and Interaction Mod[/b][/size]

An interactive, high-fidelity physical and psychological BepInEx mod for [b]Casualties: Unknown[/b] that lets you interact with your experiment subject ("expie") like a beloved, physical pet.

This mod introduces custom physics dragging/tugging forces, petting gestures, comforting neurological benefits, a satiety-fatigue simulation, and highly reactive sensory feedback depending on the subject's physical state.

[line]

[size=5][b]✨ Features[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]1. 🧲 Physical Mouse Grabbing & Tugging[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Grab and Drag:[/b] Hold the configured modifier key (default: [i]Left Alt[/i]) and [b]Left-Click[/b] on any physical limb in the world to grab and drag it.[/*]
[*][b]Spring Physics:[/b] The grabbed limb is realistically pulled towards your mouse cursor using a fully physical 2D spring-damper calculation.[/*]
[*][b]Tug-to-Flop:[/b] Grabbing and dragging a limb while the subject is standing up temporarily makes [i]only that limb[/i] dynamic and floppy, allowing you to stretch or position it while the rest of the body maintains its standing animation.[/*]
[*][b]Strain Discomfort:[/b] Dragging limbs too far beyond their range or pulling on dislocated/broken parts causes immediate localized pain spikes, tension stretch sound effects, and distressed vocalizations.[/*]
[*][b]Anti-Flying Physics Ceiling:[/b] While the subject is ragdolled, dragging targets are dynamically clamped via a downward raycast checking the [i]"Ground"[/i] layer. You can drag and slide them horizontally across any platforms, ramps, or sloped surfaces, but lifting them high into the air is blocked, neutralizing the flying physics exploit.[/*]
[/list][size=4][b]2. 🫳 Cozy Petting & Stroking[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Stroking Gesture Verification:[/b] Petting requires active hand-stroking movement above a customizable velocity threshold ([i]MinPettingSpeed[/i]). Simply hovering or holding your cursor stationary over a limb will not trigger petting.[/*]
[*][b]Soothing Neurological Benefits:[/b] Petting healthy limbs slowly heals active limb pain, increases overall character [i]happiness[/i], and reduces emotional [i]trauma/stress[/i].[/*]
[*][b]Micro-movements & Nuzzling:[/b] When petted on the head, the expie will physically lift and tilt their head into your cursor to simulate nuzzling.[/*]
[*][b]Happy Cooing:[/b] Healthy subjects express deep contentment through soft sighs, purrs, or high-pitched cozy whimpers.[/*]
[/list][size=4][b]3. 🥱 Petting Saturation & Satiety[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Per-Body Satiety Tracking:[/b] Satiety levels are tracked independently for each specific Expie body instance, allowing you to interact with multiple subjects naturally.[/*]
[*][b]Comfort Fatigue:[/b] Over-petting causes the expie to gradually grow tired of physical contact. Satiety accumulates over continuous stroking.[/*]
[*][b]Diminishing Comfort Efficiency:[/b]
[list]
[*] [b]0% to 50% Satiety:[/b] Full comfort and maximum mood boost efficiency.[/*]
[*][b]50% to 100% Satiety:[/b] Satiety sets in, smoothly scaling comfort and healing efficiency down to [i]0%[/i].[/*]
[*][b]100% Satiety (Over-stimulation):[/b] The expie is completely indifferent. No mood upgrades, pain relief, or happy vocalizations are triggered.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Persistent Moodles & Decay:[/b]
[list]
[*] [b]Satiating Petting Moodle:[/b] Once satiety reaches [i]50%[/i], the light-green [color=#66ff66][i]Satiating Petting[/i][/color] moodle remains continuously visible on the HUD, even when you stop petting, until it slowly decays back below the [i]50%[/i] threshold.[/*]
[*][b]Permanent Indifference Moodle:[/b] Once satiety reaches [i]100%[/i] (indifference is triggered), it is permanently locked at [i]100%[/i] for that specific Expie body and never decays. The orange [color=#ff9933][i]Petting Satiety[/i][/color] moodle remains visible permanently on the HUD for that subject body.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]🩹 Sensory Pain & Wounded Reactions[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]Hypersensitivity:[/b] Petting a body part with skin damage (active wounds or burns) triggers intense localized pain spikes, adrenaline shock surges, and a severe drop in happiness.[/*]
[*][b]Agonizing Vocalizations:[/b] Petting wounded parts triggers intense vocal screams and high-volume distress lines.[/*]
[*][b]Unconscious Spasms:[/b] Touching/petting active wounds on an [b]unconscious[/b] subject causes physical reflexes (sudden spasms/convulsive twitches) accompanied by low, painful groans.[/*]
[/list][line]

[size=5][b]🎮 Controls & Interaction[/b][/size]








[table][tr][td][b]Interaction[/b][/td]
[td][b]Action[/b][/td]
[td][b]Default Controls[/b][/td]
[td][b]Reactions / Effects[/b][/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Limb Tugging[/b][/td]
[td]Pull & drag body parts[/td]
[td]Hold [i]Left Alt[/i] + Click & Drag[/td]
[td]Physical stretching, joint strain, floppy standing limb dynamics, auto-clamped ground ceiling.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Cozy Petting[/b][/td]
[td]Stroke healthy parts[/td]
[td]Hold [i]Left Alt[/i] + Gentle strokes[/td]
[td]Heals limb pain, increases happiness, reduces trauma, triggers cozy sighing and happy whimpers.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Painful Petting[/b][/td]
[td]Stroke wounded parts[/td]
[td]Hold [i]Left Alt[/i] + Stroke wounds[/td]
[td]Intense pain spikes, adrenaline shock, immediate drop in happiness, screams.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Nuzzling[/b][/td]
[td]Stroke the head[/td]
[td]Hold [i]Left Alt[/i] + Stroke head[/td]
[td]Head physically lifts into your cursor, cozy purrs/coos.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]Unconscious reflex[/b][/td]
[td]Touch wounds when asleep[/td]
[td]Hold [i]Left Alt[/i] + Touch wound[/td]
[td]Subject physically twitches/groans in pain.[/td]
[/tr]
[/table][line]

[size=5][b]⚙️ Configuration[/b][/size]

A standard BepInEx config file is generated automatically at [i]BepInEx/config/expiepettingmod.casualtiesunknown.mod.cfg[/i] after launching the game once. You can customize the following settings:
[table][tr][td][b]Setting[/b][/td]
[td][b]Type[/b][/td]
[td][b]Default Value[/b][/td]
[td][b]Description[/b][/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]ModifierKey[/b][/td]
[td]KeyCode[/td]
[td][i]LeftAlt[/i][/td]
[td]The key that must be held down to drag or pet. Set to [i]None[/i] to use Left-Click directly without a modifier.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]PullStrength[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]120.0[/i][/td]
[td]How hard the mouse physically pulls/tugs body parts.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]AutoReleaseDistance[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]1.25[/i][/td]
[td]The distance a limb can be pulled before your grip automatically slips and releases.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]HappinessGainRate[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]0.3[/i][/td]
[td]How fast petting a healthy limb increases subject happiness per second.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]PainIncreaseRate[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]25.0[/i][/td]
[td]How fast petting a damaged limb spikes pain per second.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]StressDecreaseRate[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]0.5[/i][/td]
[td]How fast petting decreases trauma/stress per second.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]EnableHappyWhimpers[/b][/td]
[td]bool[/td]
[td][i]true[/i][/td]
[td]Should the subject make soft coos, sighs, and happy noises when petted?[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]MinPettingSpeed[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]120.0[/i][/td]
[td]The minimum mouse speed (in screen pixels per second) required to register petting gestures. Prevents petting while stationary.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]PettingSaturationRate[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]5.0[/i][/td]
[td]How fast petting saturation/satiety increases per second of continuous healthy petting (from 0 to 100).[/td]
[/tr]
[tr][td][b]PettingDecayRate[/b][/td]
[td]float[/td]
[td][i]2.0[/i][/td]
[td]How fast petting saturation decays per second when not being petted.[/td]
[/tr]
[/table][line]

[size=5][b]🛠️ Installation[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Ensure you have [b]BepInEx 5[/b] installed for [i]Casualties: Unknown[/i].[/*]
[*]Drop [i]ExpiePettingMod.dll[/i] into your [i]Casualties Unknown Demo/BepInEx/plugins/[/i] directory.[/*]
[*]Launch the game and enjoy interacting with your expie![/*]
[/list][line]

Author: NaeNaeTart

Name: Over The Top Realism
Description:
[size=6][b]Over-the-Top Realism Mod[/b][/size]

An unofficial, high-fidelity realism expansion for [b]Casualties Unknown (Demo)[/b].

[line]

[quote]
[size=4][b]⚠️ Unofficial Fan-Made Disclaimer[/b][/size]
This is an [b]unofficial, non-commercial, fan-made modification[/b] created purely for educational, experimental, and personal entertainment purposes.
[list]
[*] [b]No Affiliation:[/b] This project is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or in any way associated with the game's developer, [b]Orsoniks[/b], or any of the game's official publishers.[/*]
[*][b]Respecting Intellectual Property:[/b] This mod does not distribute or bundle any original game binaries, graphics, textures, audio, or other copyrighted game assets. It functions strictly as an independent code patch injected at runtime via BepInEx.[/*]
[*][b]Developer First:[/b] We deeply respect Orsoniks' work, creativity, and ownership. If you enjoy the demo, please support the official release of [i]Casualties Unknown[/i]![/*]
[/list][/quote]
[line]

[size=5][b]🩸 Overview[/b][/size]
This mod transforms the medical and survival systems of [i]Casualties Unknown[/i] into a brutal, physical, and highly realistic simulation. It expands the base game's simple stats (like cold, thirst, and pain) into tangible, real-time physical consequences. Every system is fully customizable or can be toggled off entirely in the configuration.

[line]

[size=5][b]⚙️ Core Realism Features[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]🫀 1. Cardiovascular Stroke Risk[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] High heart rate (>150 BPM) from sprinting, stress, or adrenaline builds up cardiovascular stroke risk in real-time. [/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] At critically high stroke risk (>60%), the player has a chance of experiencing sudden ventricular fibrillation (cardiac arrest).[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Cardiovascular Stroke Risk[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"stroke"[/i] sprite) warns you when your heart is under dangerous strain.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🦴 2. Traumatic Leg Fracture Strain[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Walking or jumping with a broken leg bone causes intense localized pain spikes, shock, and further degrades limb muscle health.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Continuous movement on an unsupported fracture causes internal hemorrhaging within that limb.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]💀 3. Compound Fractures[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Walking on an [i]unsplinted[/i] fractured leg has a dynamic, real-time chance of causing a [b]Compound Fracture[/b]—simulating the bone tearing through muscle and skin.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Spawns sever immediate bleeding, sets pain to maximum, drops muscle health, and causes a gut-wrenching audio snap.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Compound Leg Fracture[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"amputation"[/i] sprite) signals a severe, bleeding bone emergency.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🪵 4. Splint Fragility from Impact[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Splints are no longer permanent, invincible braces.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Taking a heavy fall or impact on a splinted leg has a high chance to snap the splint, breaking the brace and returning the bone to an unsupported or compound state.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🧠 5. Acute Pain Shock & Fainting[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] While vanilla slowly exhausts you over 20 seconds of pain, this mod adds [i]acute neurogenic shock[/i].[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Taking massive, sudden trauma that spikes pain above 75% can cause the player to immediately faint, lose consciousness, and collapse into a ragdoll on the spot.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Extreme Pain Shock[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"horrified"[/i] sprite) signals when pain levels are high enough to cause sudden blackout.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]💤 6. Sleep Deprivation Micro-Naps[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] In the base game, low energy slurs your movement but never forces you to sleep.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Attempting to walk, jump, or run when completely exhausted (Energy < 30%) carries an escalating chance of experiencing sudden, uncontrollable [b]micro-nap blackouts[/b], forcing you to collapse into sleep.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Extreme Fatigue[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"badsleep"[/i] sprite) warns when involuntary collapse is imminent.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🦠 7. Sepsis & Wound Necrosis[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Festering infected wounds are now progressively destructive.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Localized infections dynamically consume and rot away both muscle health and skin health in that limb. Systemic sepsis builds progressively over time, triggering a high core-body fever.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Systemic Septic Shock[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"dirty"[/i] sprite) tracks systemic bloodstream toxicity.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]💫 8. Concussions & Head Trauma Vertigo[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Heavy head impacts or severe head pain accumulate a custom concussion level.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Concussions cause physical head/camera jitter, momentary reversed movement controls (vertigo), and severe jump-induced vertigo blackouts.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Concussive Vertigo[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"braindamage"[/i] sprite) tracks head trauma.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]⚡ 9. Electrolyte Cramps & Item Drops[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Severe dehydration (Thirst < 25%) triggers skeletal and muscular cramps.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Leg cramps trigger pain spikes and instantly trip/ragdoll you. Hand cramps trigger pain in the arms and force-drop whatever weapon or item you are holding in your active slot.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Dehydration Cramps[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"underweight"[/i] sprite) warns you before spasms strike.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]❄️ 10. Hypothermia & Frostbite Necrosis[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Core cold (< 35°C) doubles stamina drainage and introduces physical shivering forces to all limbs.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Extreme cold (< 32°C) introduces severe arm/aim sway. Freezing cold (< 30°C) triggers frostbite cell necrosis, decaying non-vital extremity muscle and skin health.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Severe Hypothermia[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"wet"[/i] sprite) tracks shivering and frostbite decay.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🫁 11. Suffocation Panic[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] Low blood oxygen (< 65%) triggers adrenaline surges, heart rate spikes, and visible hyperventilating bodily vibration.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Oxygen below 40% drains consciousness rapidly, leading to asphyxiation blackouts.[/*]
[*][b]Moodle:[/b] [color=#ff6666][i]Suffocation Panic[/i][/color] (uses the [i]"oxygen"[/i] sprite) signals oxygen distress.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]🩸 12. Hemodynamics & Rest-Dependent Clotting[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]The Mechanic:[/b] High blood pressure (>120) physically pumps blood out of wounds faster.[/*]
[*][b]The Threat:[/b] Active movement, running, jumping, or flopping on the ground negates 75% to 100% of the body's natural wound clotting. Staying still or resting is now mandatory for wounds to close naturally.[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[size=5][b]💊 13. Drug Realism & Mixed Interactions[/b][/size]

This system prevents players from using drugs as consequence-free, magical health cures.

[list]
[*][b]Opioid Respiratory Depression:[/b] High painkiller levels ([i]opiateAmount > 60 mg[/i]) depress the central nervous system, actively throttling your respiratory rate. Severe overdoses ([i]opiateAmount > 150 mg[/i]) cause sudden blackouts and direct blood oxygen loss (asphyxiation) unless treated with Naloxone (which vanilla models via [i]antagonistAmount[/i]).
[i]Moodle:[/i] [color=#ff6666]Painkiller Toxicity[/color] (uses the [i]"brainhealth"[/i] sprite) tracks dosage safety.[/*]
[*][b]Lethal Drug Interaction:[/b] Mixing painkillers ([i]opiateAmount > 20 mg[/i]) and sleeping pills ([i]amount > 30[/i]) triggers [b]synergistic CNS depression[/b], multiplying respiratory failure by [b]3x[/b] and causing a rapid, life-threatening blackout and suffocation.
[i]Moodle:[/i] [color=#ff6666]Lethal Drug Interaction[/color] (uses the [i]"stroke"[/i] sprite) warns of mixed-pill arrest.[/*]
[*][b]Opioid Withdrawal Tremors:[/b] Going through withdrawal ([i]actualOpiateReception < -10%[/i]) triggers severe shivering (cold sweats) and heavy arm aim shake (torque jolts), alongside temperature instability.
[i]Moodle:[/i] [color=#ff6666]Opiate Withdrawal Chills[/color] (uses the [i]"cough"[/i] sprite) tracks detoxification.[/*]
[*][b]Stimulant Hyper-Toxicity & Crash:[/b] Abusing caffeine or adrenaline surges while under physical strain causes violent cardiac arrhythmia and stroke risk spikes. Once stimulants wear off, you suffer a severe energy crash.
[i]Moodle:[/i] [color=#ff6666]Stimulant Over-Toxicity[/color] (uses the [i]"stroke"[/i] sprite) / [color=#ff6666]Stimulant Crash[/color] (uses the [i]"badsleep"[/i] sprite).[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[size=5][b]🔌 Installation & Customization[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Ensure you have [b]BepInEx[/b] installed for [i]Casualties Unknown Demo[/i].[/*]
[*]Copy [i]OverTheTopRealism.dll[/i] into the [i]BepInEx/plugins/[/i] directory.[/*]
[*]Start the game once to generate the configuration file.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]Customization[/b][/size]
Every feature can be customized or fully disabled in the generated config file:
📄 [i]BepInEx/config/OverTheTopRealism.cfg[/i]

[line]

[size=5][b]📄 License[/b][/size]

Author: NaeNaeTart

Name: Thunder
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[size=3]⚡ Thunder is an in-game performance profiler for Casualties Unknown, built on BepInEx + Harmony. It samples frame time, GC activity, world state, engine-component CPU cost, multiplayer network metrics, and the per-Update cost of every loaded BepInEx plugin. Read it live through the graphical [b]HUD[/b] overlay (press [b]F1[/b]), the in-game console command [b]thunder[/b], or run [b]thunder upload[/b] to capture a window and POST a JSON snapshot to a Thunder web viewer for sharing with mod authors and bug reporters.[/size]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]

1. Install [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases]BepInEx 5.x[/url] for the game (drop it next to [b]CasualtiesUnknown.exe[/b] and run the game once so it generates the [b]BepInEx[/b] folder).
2. Download the latest Thunder release.
3. Extract [b]Thunder.dll[/b] into:
[b]<GameDir>\BepInEx\plugins\Thunder.dll[/b]
4. Launch the game. You should see "[b][Thunder] ready. Try thunder or thunder help."[/b] in the game console.
5. Press [b]F1[/b] for the HUD, or open the console and type [b]thunder help[/b] to verify it's registered.

Optional config (auto-generated on first launch at [b]BepInEx\config\com.kanisuko.thunder.cfg[/b]):
[list]
[*][size=3][b]Upload.Endpoint[/b] — where snapshots are POSTed (default points at the public Thunder viewer).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Upload.Token[/b] — optional [b]X-Thunder-Token[/b] header for private endpoints.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Upload.AutoCopyUrl[/b] / [b]Upload.AutoOpenBrowser[/b] — UX after upload.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Profiler.ProfileOtherMods[/b] — patch every other plugin's Update/LateUpdate/FixedUpdate to attribute frame cost per mod GUID. Tiny per-call overhead; turn off if you suspect interference.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Profiler.EnableComponentCpu[/b] / [b]ComponentCpuPlayerLoopFallback[/b] — engine-stage CPU timing via Unity Recorder, with a PlayerLoop fallback.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Profiler.DownsampleSections[/b] — time game/system sections once every 0.5s to reduce profiler self-overhead (counts stay per-frame).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]HUD.*[/b] — overlay scale, corner anchor, tooltips, GL chart, smoothing, refresh interval, and per-panel toggles.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Keybinds.ToggleHud[/b] / [b]ToggleSettings[/b] — rebindable F1 / F2 hotkeys.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Net.EnableNetBridge[/b] — reflection-based multiplayer metrics; auto-disables silently in single-player.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=3][b]Graphical HUD overlay[/b] — on-screen panels with a live GL frame-time chart (30/60fps reference lines), 4-corner anchoring, scaling, hover tooltips, and an in-game settings panel (F2). Plain-text HUD fallback included.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Live frame stats[/b] — avg / best / worst FPS, frame time, and 1% low computed over a configurable last-N-seconds window, backed by a rolling 300-frame history.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Engine-component CPU timing[/b] — per-stage cost for Scripts / Physics2D / Rendering / Animation / UI / Audio / GC via Unity Recorder, with a PlayerLoop-injection fallback when Recorder reads zero.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]GPU & memory view[/b] — GPU/main-thread frame time, process CPU%, managed heap, per-frame g0/g1/g2 deltas, cumulative GC counts, Mono heap, Unity allocator, system RAM/VRAM.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Multiplayer network metrics[/b] — ping, connection quality, up/down bandwidth, packets, and peer ping when the multiplayer mod is present (soft dependency; single-player safe).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]System timings (55+ Sections)[/b] — deep-dive profiling across 55+ subsystems: 35+ PlayerCamera Handle* methods, 9 WorldGeneration sub-methods, Moodles (Update vs events), Fluid simulation, Body updates, and visible Chunk entity ticks.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Per-mod profiler[/b] — automatically times Update / LateUpdate / FixedUpdate of every loaded BepInEx plugin and lists the top offenders by avg ms, with friendly plugin names.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]World snapshot[/b] — live entity count, accurate visible chunks, real-time spider count (decrements on kill), current biome layer, and player position.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]One-shot upload[/b] — [b]thunder upload [seconds][/b] clears history, captures a clean window (≤ 600 s), builds a self-contained JSON snapshot (meta + frame history + sections + mods), POSTs it, and optionally copies the result URL and opens it in your browser.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Console UX[/b] — color-coded output (green/yellow/red thresholds), ASCII bars, autofill for sub-commands, and a [b]thunder debug[/b] self-check for diagnosing the sampler itself.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Console commands[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=3][b]thunder help[/b] — list all sub-commands.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder fps[/b] — FPS, frame time, 1% low (last-N-seconds window).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder frame[/b] — detailed frame stats (avg/min/max + timeScale).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder health[/b] — quick overview (FPS + GC + world counts).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder profiler[/b] — per-system and per-mod avg/max ms + calls/frame.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder gc[/b] — managed heap and GC deltas.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder mem[/b] — Unity heap, Mono heap, allocator info.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder net [reprobe][/b] — multiplayer ping/bandwidth/packets; 'reprobe' re-resolves the bridge.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder world[/b] — entities, chunks, spiders, layer, player pos.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder reset[/b] — clear sample history.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder upload [sec][/b] — capture for [sec] seconds, then upload a snapshot.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]thunder debug[/b] — sampler self-check.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=3][b]Casualties Unknown[/b] (tested against the current Demo build).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]BepInEx 5.x[/b] (ships with HarmonyX / 0Harmony, which Thunder uses for all patches).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]No other mods required. Thunder is a pure observer — it never modifies game behavior, only times it. Multiplayer metrics are optional and activate only if a supported MP mod is installed.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Changelog (v1.2.0)[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=3][b]Graphical HUD[/b]: New on-screen overlay with a live GL frame-time chart, 4-corner anchoring, scaling, hover tooltips, and an F2 settings panel with rebindable hotkeys (F1 toggles HUD).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Engine-Component CPU Timing[/b]: Per-stage profiling (Scripts/Physics2D/Rendering/Animation/UI/Audio/GC) via Unity Recorder, with a PlayerLoop-injection fallback for unmatched pipelines.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Multiplayer Network Metrics[/b]: New [b]thunder net[/b] command and HUD panel — ping, bandwidth, packets, and peer ping via a soft reflection bridge (single-player safe).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]GPU / CPU Metrics[/b]: Added GPU and main-thread frame time plus process CPU% capture.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Time-Window Stats[/b]: FPS/1% low now computed over a configurable last-N-seconds window; frame history extended from 120 to 300 frames.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Sampling Downsample[/b]: Optional mode times sections once every 0.5s to cut profiler self-overhead (counts stay per-frame).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Display Smoothing[/b]: Exponential smoothing for steadier on-screen numbers; raw upload data is never smoothed.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3][b]Fixes[/b]: Reworked chunk-visibility timing (now reads the TilemapRenderer field directly), single-commit VisibleChunks count, more accurate 1% low, low-priority console registration (no longer risks wiping vanilla commands), and unified version metadata.[/size][/*]
[/list]
Author: QinShenYu and Kanisuko

Name: The X'i Race (WIP)
Description:
The mod description is machine-translated, so there may be some unclear or awkward phrasing... I'm really sorry about that!! ><

[b][size=4]Installation instructions[/size][/b]
Extract and put all files into  your BepInEx/plugins folder

[b][size=4]Requirements[/size][/b]
BepInEx
GSR's General Framework


[b](This race supports customsprite and color customization! If you want to know how to configure it, please visit the mod page for [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/327]GSR's General Framework[/url].)[/b]

[color=#D4D4D8][b]Base Traits:[/b][/color]

[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Stamina regeneration rate: ×1.2[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Hungry cap: 200[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Thirst cap: 300[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Immune to radiation[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Immune to vomiting caused by food or drink[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Will not get sick from eating spoiled/contaminated food[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]No movement speed penalty or blood pressure increase from overeating or overdrinking[/color][/*]
[/list][b]While near a radiation source:[/b]
[list]
[*]Damage multiplier: ×1.15[/*]
[*]Stamina regen: additional ×1.15 (stacking with base)[/*]
[*]Hunger rate: ×0.95[/*]
[/list][b]Hunger/Thirst drain:[/b]
1.25× than Expie. If body temperature > 37.2°C, this multiplier increases to 1.35×.

[b]Temperature:[/b]
Normal and high‑threshold temperatures are 0.5°C lower than Expie's.

[b]Miscellaneous:[/b]
[list]
[*]Cannot use bandage‑type items to treat wounds[/*]
[*]Cannot speak[/*]
[*]Mood: –2 extra decrease when seeing a corpse, –5 extra when killing a merchant[/*]
[*]Weight always decreases at twice the rate of Expie; satiety does not affect this rate.[/*]
[/list][b]Active Crouch Mechanics:[/b]
[list]
[*]While manually crouching, consumes 1.5 satiety per second.[/*]
[*]After crouching for 8–10 seconds consecutively, consumes 12–15 satiety total and spawns 1–2 [b]X'ian Seed Bulbs[/b].
[list]
[*]19% chance (9% if Hunger < 50) to spawn a rarer variant, and 1% chance for a even rarer variants.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[/list][b]X'ian Seed/Mutant/Alpha Bulb items:[/b]
[list]
[*]Each second, durability of these items is consumed to restore muscle/skin health and reduce bleeding.[/*]
[*]The game prioritises the rarest item with the lowest durability first.[/*]
[*]Each tier (Mutant → Alpha) has twice the healing effect of the previous tier.[/*]
[*]when dropped on the ground, turns into a X'ian Bulb after 1–2 minutes.[/*]
[*]This is a key crafting material for most of the race's unique items.[/*]
[*]If a non‑X'i race holds any X'ian Bulb series item, they will suffer radiation damage.[/*]
[/list][b]Crafting & Content:[/b]
Includes several unique recipes, items, and weapons.

still in early development, so expect some weird bugs.
[b]Balance note:[/b] I usually play with heavily overpowered/cheat‑friendly mods, so the numbers here might feel a bit under‑tuned for some players. Apologies for I'm not great at balancing...><
[b]Special thanks / Origin:[/b]
Inspired by the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=729480149]Frackin' Universe mod[/url] for [i]Starbound[/i].

Most of the textures and race designs are sourced from the Frackin' Universe mod. The original mod's GitHub page states that it is licensed under CC BY 4.0. So I guess that means I'm allowed to do this.. ^^ If still any copyright issues arise, I will take this mod down.
Author: GeeserGSR

Name: GSR's General Framework (WIP)
Description:
[b][size=4]Installation instructions[/size][/b]
[color=#D4D4D8]Place the .dll file into your BepInEx/plugins folder [/color]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]This framework provides modders with the ability to create:
[/color]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom tiles[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Multiple variants of the same tile[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom structures[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom items[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Liquid mixing recipes (automatic mixing in containers)[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Liquid transformation recipes[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Item idle / dual-state animations[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Charge-up item usage[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom hooks[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom objects[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom one-way platforms[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom blood colors[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom base stat adjustments[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom appearances (with independent sprites for both arms and legs)[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Color palettes for the same sprite set[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom races[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Custom recipes (with the option to restrict certain recipes to specific races)[/color][/*]
[/list]After installing this framework, you'll see one or more buttons in the upper-left corner of the main menu. If you have a race mod built on this framework, you can press F1 to configure custom color palettes for each race (if available). In-game, press number keys 5–0 to switch sprites for: front hair / head / upper body / lower body / tail / back hair respectively.
(Note: Hair sprites should be replaced while facing right and standing straight — otherwise you may encounter minor visual glitches! Since this doesn't significantly affect gameplay, I haven't gotten around to fixing it...)

The framework is still a work in progress, so I'm not planning to release the source code or a tutorial just yet... If you'd like to try making an add-on mod with this framework, be prepared to spend some time getting used to its structure.

Compatible with Rshlib, but currently [b]not compatible with QoL Unknown's save function or with the Multiplayer Mod.[/b]

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]BepInEX[/color]
Author: GeeserGSR

Name: Enhanced Titanium Pickaxe
Description:
[list]
[*][size=4][b] Description 简述[/b][/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]——————————[b]CN[/b]—————————— [/size]
[color=#d4d4d8][font=Comic Sans MS]你利用了钛合金多功能工具将这把工具又上升了一个层次：将镐尖打磨得更锋利，无论在何种场景下它都更适用。并且利用了剩下的材料做了握把，这使得它的操控性更好，更迅速，更省力，更致命。结合了多功能工具的优势，并且底部粘合的红磷和玻璃粉可以当作更适合的引火器。 [/font][/color]
[color=#00ffff][font=Comic Sans MS]你需要智力17才能制造此工具。[/font][/color]
[b][size=3][color=#00ffff][font=Comic Sans MS][i]你不能在没有电池的情况下使用它，然而，有电池的情况下，内置的充电器会自动充能，你也可以把它当成一个自动充电器。[/i][/font][/color][/size][/b]
[size=4]——————————[b]EN[/b]——————————[/size]
[font=Verdana]You've taken this tool to the next level using a titanium multi-tool. You sharpen the pickaxe tip to a finer edge, making it more effective in any situation. With the leftover materials, you craft a handle that improves control — faster, requires less stamina, and deadlier. [i]Combining the versatility of the multi-tool[/i], you also attach a mix of red phosphorus and glass powder to the base, which serves as a more reliable fire starter.[/font]
[color=#00ffff][font=Verdana]INT must be 17 to craft.
[b][i]The tool can't function without battery.However,the built‑in device slowly recharges your battery automatically after installed. [/i][/b][/font][/color]

[color=#c27ba0]---materials to craft：[/color]
[spoiler][b]Titanium Pickaxe -（钛镐）
Multi-tool -（多用途工具）
Hand crank charger -（手摇充电器）
Circuit board x2（90%condition）- 电路板(90%耐久以上)
Bundle of wires x3 -（电线捆x3）
Fire starter -（引火器）
Biochem fluid 50ml -（生化流体50ml）[/b][/spoiler]

Name: Gunsaw Genetics
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]Gunsaw Genetics adds all 14 (+ Albino) species to the game for you to be able to use during gameplay. [/color]

[left][size=6][b][color=#ff0000]NOTE: SPRITES ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THIS MOD. DOWNLOAD THEM FROM HERE:[/color][color=#d4d4d8] [/color][/b] [url=https://skin.cat-bot.de/][b][color=#ff00ff]https://skin.cat-bot.de/[/color][/b][/url][/size][/left]




[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/4fOFweo.png[/img][/center]
[center]Is this mod balanced? [i]No, not at all.[/i][/center]


[b][size=5]Installation instructions:[/size][/b]
[color=#D4D4D8]Requirements:
[b]BepInEx must be installed first.
[/b][/color][b][color=#d4d4d8]Game version: Casualties Unknown demo v7.0[/color][/b]

[color=#d4d4d8][b]
[size=5]Steps:[/size][/b][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][b][size=4]1.[/size][/b] Download the Gunsaw Genetics.zip file.[/color]

[color=#d4d4d8][b][size=4]2.[/size][/b] Extract the contents of the .zip file.[/color]

[color=#d4d4d8][b][size=4]3.[/size][/b] Drag and drop the contents of the Gunsaw Genetics folder directly into your BepInEx plugins folder. [/color]
[b][color=#d4d4d8]Overwrite if you are using the original Player sprite replacer.[/color][/b]

[b][i][color=#d4d4d8]Do not put the Gunsaw Genetics folder [/color][color=#ff0000]itself[/color][color=#d4d4d8] into your plugins folder.[/color]
[/i][/b][b]
If you already have CustomSprites installed from a previous version of Gunsaw Genetics (Meaning, separated into Head / Body already) you [color=#ff0000]do not[/color] have to replace it. Doing so will revert your sprites to the placeholder ones.[/b]


[i][color=#d4d4d8]This is the correct format:[/color]
[/i][color=#d4d4d8]BepInEx/plugins/GunsawGenetics.dll[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]BepInEx/plugins/body_sprite_replacer.dll[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]etc.[/color]


[size=4][color=#d4d4d8](Optional but [/color][b][color=#d4d4d8]HIGHLY[/color][/b][color=#d4d4d8] Recommended)[/color][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]To add custom skins, navigate to \BepInEx\plugins\CustomSprites and add in the sprites of your choosing. Folders are ordered the same as the species. 1 = Expie, 15 = Albino. [b]Make sure to properly separate Head and Body sprites.[/b][/color]

[color=#d4d4d8]It [/color][i][color=#d4d4d8]might[/color][/i][color=#d4d4d8] work with everything in Body, but separating is the most reliable.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][u]Placeholder sprites are added with the proper names and placements.[/u][/color]

[color=#d4d4d8][b][size=4]4.[/size][/b] Launch the game.[/color]

[size=4][b]Main features:[/b][/size]

[color=#d4d4d8]Each species is given different stats, resistances, passives, actives, and sometimes even items.[/color]

[size=3][color=#d4d4d8]As of v1.1.0, progression has been added to unlocking each species. This is[/color] [b][color=#ff00ff]entirely optional[/color][color=#d4d4d8].[/color][/b][color=#d4d4d8] There is a checkbox on the same menu that can be used to unlock every species. It can be toggled at any time and does [/color][i][color=#d4d4d8]not[/color][/i][color=#d4d4d8] wipe progress.[/color][/size]

[i][color=#d4d4d8]These are definitely not final. This is my first mod outside of Minecraft, so I'm still learning. [/color][/i]
[i][color=#d4d4d8]Expect new mechanics, abilities, etc. to be added with time.[/color][/i]


[left][b][color=#ff7700][size=4]Experiment:[/size][/color][/b][/left]
[left][color=#d4d4d8]STR = 9, RES = 8, INT = 7.[/color][/left]
[left][color=#d4d4d8]Unchanged.[/color][/left]
[left][color=#d4d4d8][i][size=2]The one we all come from?[/size][/i][/color][/left]

[color=#b45f06][size=4][b]Orange: [/b][/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 12, RES = 11, INT = 4.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Starts with limb wraps and a candy bar.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][size=2][i]Brute. Dumb as rock, and about as durable.[/i][/size][/color]


[spoiler][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]Reach Layer 2[/size][/color][/spoiler]

[color=#ffffff][size=4][b]Milky: [/b][/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 5, RES = 4, INT = 12.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][s]Starts with combat pen and an electronic watch.[/s] Overhauled in v1.3.0. Check changelog.[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]Well equipped. Not the best company.[/size][/color]
[/i]
[spoiler]Seek out the physical records left behind.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#38761d][size=4]Leapy: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 10, RES = 5, INT = 6.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Dash ability with Left Alt. Hyper regen.[/color]
[size=2][color=#d4d4d8][i]Incredibly fast. Show-offs.[/i][/color][/size]

[spoiler]Experience catastrophic, simultaneous bipedal fractures.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#1155cc][size=4]Chompy: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 10, RES = 10, INT = 7.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Water breathing + mobility.[/color]

[spoiler]Starve the brain of oxygen until the absolute brink of oblivion.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#6d9eeb][size=4]Roza: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 9, RES = 9, INT = 8.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Starts with an icepack and frigiant fruit. Cold resistance.[/color]
[size=2][color=#d4d4d8][i]Naturally colder than the rest. Not much else.[/i][/color][/size]

[spoiler]Core temperature must reach absolute failure.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#9900ff][size=4]Voyager: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 8, RES = 6, INT = 10.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Stand still to cloak.[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]"Amateurs... All of them. Hardly even an obstacle."[/size][/color][/i]

[spoiler]Traverse an entire layer without shedding a single drop of blood.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#a61c00][size=4]Baron: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 14, RES = 14, INT = 5.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Absolute tank. No natural healing. Regular medical treatment is still effective.[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]"You're up against the wall, and I am the wall!"[/size][/color][/i]

[spoiler]Reach Layer 5[/spoiler]


[b][color=#ff00ff][size=4]Chik: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 8, RES = 6, INT = 10.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Air jumps and slow fall. Hold S to fall normally.[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]Look up. Though, you'll probably hear the bastards anyways.[/size][/color][/i]

[spoiler]Execute a hostile target while suspended by jet propulsion.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#00ffff][size=4]Crystal: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 9, RES = 8, INT = 6.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Shoot tongue out with Left Alt. Acts like a grappling hook. [/color]
[size=2][color=#d4d4d8][i]Highly useful biology for movement, and... reptilian.[/i][/color][/size]

[spoiler]Reach Layer 3[/spoiler]


[b][color=#ff0000][size=4]Velvet: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 10, RES = 11, INT = 6.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Starts with an old rag and sleeping pills.[/color]
[size=2][color=#d4d4d8][i]Sadistic and psychotic. Don't listen to them.[/i][/color][/size]

[spoiler]Reach Layer 4[/spoiler]


[b][color=#e06666][size=4]Icke: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 11, RES = 8, INT = 5.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Enrage as health decreases.[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]It won't save you from death. It will just keep you moving.[/size][/color][/i]

[spoiler]Execute a hostile target while clinging to your own fading life.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#ffff00][size=4]Shelly: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 8, RES = 14, INT = 7.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Heavy explosion resistance. Immune to fire damage.[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]Highly durable exoskeleton. Covers enough surface area to render them mute.[/size][/color][/i]

[spoiler]Stand inside the blast radius and walk out alive.[/spoiler]


[color=#f6b26b][size=4][b]Dune: [/b][/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 10, RES = 6, INT = 3.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Water breathing, no emotions, sickness resistance.[/color]
[size=2][color=#d4d4d8][i]Driven purely by hunger. Even the talkative ones are still thinking about how you'd taste.[/i][/color][/size]

[spoiler]The shifting sands hide old texts. Find what was written.[/spoiler]


[b][color=#ffffff][size=4]Albino: [/size][/color][/b]
[color=#d4d4d8]STR = 7, RES = 6, INT = 6.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Starts with painkillers, hoodie, and a scarf. Has a Left Alt ability. Be careful...[/color]
[i][color=#d4d4d8][size=2]Again. We must go again. Further this time.[/size][/color][/i]

[spoiler][i]REACH OUT TO THE OTHERS AND I WILL RETURN AGAIN[/i][/spoiler]

[color=#D4D4D8]Again...

[b]BepInEx must be installed first.[/b] [/color]
